free_text: Measurement of gamma-ray production cross sections for
nuclear reactions 14N(d,pg)15N and 28Si(d,pg)29Si
A thin film of (Six)(Ny) x = 1, y = 1 +- 0.05,
thickness: 200 +- 14 nm, area: 5x5mm2
from Norcada Inc.
for gamma measurement at 55 degree.
for particle measurement at 135 degree.
Prompt gammas were detected at 55 degree.
A single target was irradiated with different
energy deuteron beam.
Gamma-rays, protons and backscattered deuterons were
detected simultaneously.
Cross section measurements were carried out in 50 keV
steps from 2 to 0.65 MeV, and at certain points the
measurement was repeated in two additional runs.
Beam intensity was between 20 and 50 nA.
Measured angular anisotropy of gammas at 1.95 MeV.
No value is given.
Uncertainty propagated from:
Stochastic plus systematic error of beam
intensity: 3%. Stochastic uncertainty of the beam
integration was less than 1%
Number of target atoms: 2.5%
Detector efficiency: 2%
Gamma background was less than 2.5%
ts
Cross sections for
production of 1885 keV gamma from the 7155keV level.
The total cross sections of the present work were
obtained by multiplying the differential cross section
measured at 55 degree by 4Pi.
Statistical error for nitrogen was better
than 4%
Numerical data were provided by author.
